task.name: format-adapt

metadata.description: Adapt a primary SSD to a target format by filtering scenes by priority, adjusting timing, applying crop strategies, and inserting platform-specific hooks and CTAs.

input.name: target_format
input.description: Target format preset identifier (e.g., youtube_long, youtube_short, instagram_reel, tiktok, twitter_clip, linkedin_video, thumbnail, trailer).

input.name: format_presets
input.description: Configuration file defining all format presets with their constraints and rules.

output.description: Derived SSD tailored to the target format's constraints.

step.name: Load Inputs
step.description: Load primary SSD and target format configuration.

actions.action: Read and parse episodes/{episode_id}/ssd/scene-spec.yaml
Read and parse _cookie/_config/format-presets.yaml
Validate that target_format exists in format-presets.yaml

Extract format preset fields: - aspect_ratio (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:5) - max_duration (seconds) - min_duration (seconds) - resolution - crop_strategy (center-crop, center-subject, letterbox, pan-scan) - requires_hook (boolean) - hook_max_duration (seconds) - requires_cta (boolean) - cta_position (end, overlay) - cta_max_duration (seconds) - priority_filter: [essential, important, supplementary] - pacing_multiplier (float)

step.name: Calculate Time Budget
step.description: Determine the available time for content scenes after reserving time for hook and CTA.

actions.action: Start with max_duration from the format preset
If requires_hook == true: subtract hook_max_duration
If requires_cta == true AND cta_position == "end": subtract cta_max_duration
available_content_time = max_duration - hook_time - cta_time

step.name: Filter Scenes by Priority
step.description: Select scenes based on priority tiers and available time budget.

actions.action:
Each scene in the SSD has a priority field: essential, important, or supplementary.
Phase 1: Include all "essential" scenes. Calculate running_duration. Phase 2: If running_duration < available_content_time, add "important" scenes in order. Phase 3: If still room, add "supplementary" scenes in order.
Stop adding when the next scene would exceed available_content_time.
If essential scenes alone exceed available_content_time: - Flag WARNING: essential content exceeds time budget - Proceed to timing adjustment step
Record excluded scenes with reasons: - "{scene_id}: excluded -- priority '{priority}' not included for {target_format}" - "{scene_id}: excluded -- exceeded time budget"

step.name: Adjust Timing
step.description: Compress or adjust scene durations to fit the time budget.

actions.action: total_selected_duration = sum of selected scene durations If total_selected_duration <= available_content_time: no adjustment needed.

If total_selected_duration > available_content_time: Apply tiered trimming — never reduce essential scenes:
Tier 1: Remove supplementary scenes (lowest priority first) until within budget. Tier 2: If still over budget, remove important scenes (lowest priority first). Tier 3: If only essential scenes remain and still over budget: - Apply proportional scaling ONLY to essential scenes as last resort - scale_factor = available_content_time / total_essential_duration - For each essential scene: adjusted_duration = original_duration * scale_factor - Snap to valid VEO durations: [4, 5, 6, 7, 8] (pick closest valid value) - Flag WARNING: "Essential scenes compressed by {percent}%" - Verify total adjusted duration fits within available_content_time
Apply pacing_multiplier if set: - If pacing_multiplier > 1.0: scenes should feel faster (shorter transitions) - If pacing_multiplier < 1.0: scenes should feel slower (longer holds)

step.name: Apply Crop Strategy
step.description: Annotate each scene with the appropriate crop strategy for aspect ratio conversion.

actions.action: Determine source vs target aspect ratio: - If source == target: crop_strategy = "none" - If conversion needed, apply the preset's default crop_strategy

Available crop strategies: - center-crop: Crop from center, losing edges. Best for generic scenes. - center-subject: Crop centered on primary subject. Best for character scenes. - letterbox: Add black bars to preserve full frame. Preserves all content. - pan-scan: Animated pan across the wider frame. Best for establishing shots.
Per-scene override logic: - Scenes with characters: prefer center-subject - Scenes with wide landscapes: prefer pan-scan - Screen recordings: prefer center-crop - Text-heavy scenes: prefer letterbox

Generate crop calculations and FFmpeg filter values per scene.
Example — center-crop from 16:9 (1920x1080) to 1:1 (1080x1080): crop_x = (1920 - 1080) / 2 = 420 crop_y = 0 crop_width = 1080 crop_height = 1080 ffmpeg_filter = "crop=1080:1080:420:0"
Example — center-crop from 16:9 (1920x1080) to 9:16 (608x1080): crop_x = (1920 - 608) / 2 = 656 crop_y = 0 crop_width = 608 crop_height = 1080 ffmpeg_filter = "crop=608:1080:656:0"
Example — letterbox from 16:9 (1920x1080) to 1:1 (1080x1080): scale_width = 1080 scale_height = 607 pad_top = (1080 - 607) / 2 = 236 pad_bottom = 1080 - 607 - 236 = 237 ffmpeg_filter = "scale=1080:-2,pad=1080:1080:0:236:black"
Example — letterbox from 16:9 (1920x1080) to 9:16 (1080x1920): scale_width = 1080 scale_height = 607 pad_top = (1920 - 607) / 2 = 656 pad_bottom = 1920 - 607 - 656 = 657 ffmpeg_filter = "scale=1080:-2,pad=1080:1920:0:656:black"
Example — center-subject: Same as center-crop but offset crop_x/crop_y based on subject position from SSD character_refs or scene description.
Example — pan-scan: Generate keyframe pairs at scene start/end: keyframe_start = { crop_x: 0, crop_y: 0 } keyframe_end = { crop_x: 840, crop_y: 0 } ffmpeg_filter = "crop=1080:1080:'420+420*t/{duration}':0"
Add crop_strategy, crop_metadata, and ffmpeg_filter to each scene in the derived SSD

step.name: Reposition Text Overlays
step.description: Adjust text overlay positions and sizes for the target aspect ratio's safe zones.

actions.action: For each text overlay in the scene: - Recalculate x and y positions to fit within the target safe zone - If the text was at "center" in 16:9, keep it centered in the new ratio - If the text was positioned at specific coordinates, scale proportionally to the new dimensions - Ensure no text extends beyond the title safe zone boundaries

Scale font sizes proportionally to the new aspect ratio: - For 16:9 to 9:16 conversion: reduce font size by approximately 15-20% (narrower frame) - For 16:9 to 1:1 conversion: reduce font size by approximately 10% (less horizontal space) - Ensure minimum readable size is maintained (no smaller than 24px at 1080p equivalent)

For captions and subtitles: - In 9:16: position in the caption_zone (60-85% from top) to avoid platform UI overlays - In 1:1: position in lower 20% of frame - In 16:9: position in lower 10% of frame (standard subtitle position)

step.name: Add Hook and CTA
step.description: Insert hook and call-to-action elements if required by the social format.

actions.action: If requires_hook == true: - Create a hook scene at position 0 - Duration: hook_max_duration - Source: derive from the most visually impactful essential scene - Purpose: attention-grabbing opening for {format_name}

If requires_cta == true: - If cta_position == "end": append CTA as final scene with cta_max_duration - If cta_position == "overlay": add CTA as overlay metadata on the final content scene - Content: subscribe/follow/like prompt appropriate for the target platform

If add_captions == true (or target format requires always-on captions): - Enable subtitles on every scene: set remotion.subtitles.enabled = true - Set subtitle style appropriate for target platform: - 9:16 (TikTok/Reels): style = "tiktok-center" - 1:1 (Instagram feed): style = "minimal-bottom" - 16:9 (YouTube): style = "minimal-bottom" - If narration text exists per scene, use it as subtitle source - If no narration, flag scene for manual subtitle entry

step.name: Write Derived SSD
step.description: Write the adapted SSD to the format output directory.

actions.action: Ensure episodes/{episode_id}/formats/{target_format}/ directory exists
Write scene-spec.yaml with the derived content

error.action: HALT: "Primary scene specification not found at episodes/{episode_id}/ssd/scene-spec.yaml"
HALT: "Format preset '{target_format}' not found in _cookie/_config/format-presets.yaml. Available: {list}"
HALT: "No scenes with 'essential' priority found. Cannot create format adaptation."
WARN: "Essential scenes total {X}s exceeds max_duration {Y}s. Applying aggressive timing compression."
WARN: "Scene {scene_id} has no priority field. Defaulting to 'supplementary'."
